layout_authority.artifact_name: AI_Harness_Prompt_Kit_v33 - Locked Tabs - Need Link to the Prompt Range in each Prompt Tab.xlsx
layout_authority.policy: Preserve the manual V33 order/colors/protection, insert P45-P47 after P44, and add prompt-range recovery links.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Convert repo evidence and operator intent into one executable GNHF prompt
prompts.use_this_when: An AI assistant has the conversation and repository context but Goodnight Have Fun needs one bounded, paste-ready execution prompt.
prompts.inspect_first: Repo rules, current branch/worktrees, open PRs, plans, validators, artifacts, exact agent spawn evidence, and operator constraints.
prompts.expected_output: Exactly one self-contained GNHF launch command or prompt panel with bounded scope, validation, commit proof, stop conditions, and proof ceiling.
prompts.next_step: Run agent spawn preflight, then paste the generated command into the proven GNHF route.
prompts.proof_gate: The output names repo, branch/worktree, agent, caps, owned and forbidden scope, artifacts, validators, commit contract, stop condition, and final response contract.
lines: Generate one paste-ready Goodnight Have Fun prompt for the repository task below. Do not execute the sprint and do not return a plan without the runnable prompt.
INPUTS
Repo: xyz_repo_or_path
Sprint: xyz_sprint_name
Operator goal: xyz_operator_goal
Known branch, PR, worktree, or plan: xyz_known_context
Agent route: xyz_proven_gnhf_agent_spec
INSPECT BEFORE WRITING
Use repository evidence when available: AGENTS.md, current status and branch, recent commits, worktrees, open PRs, plans, manifests, validators, tests, artifacts, logs, and CI.
Mark stale or unavailable evidence explicitly. Do not invent repository facts.
OUTPUT CONTRACT
Return exactly one self-contained GNHF command or copyable prompt panel.
The generated prompt must instruct GNHF to inspect, modify tracked files, validate, commit, and stop with evidence.

Include repo and local path, branch or isolated worktree, agent spec, maximum iterations, maximum tokens, sleep prevention, deterministic stop condition, owned scope, forbidden scope, expected artifacts, read-first targets, numbered tasks, validation order, commit and push contract, proof level, proof ceiling, exact final response, and exact next command.
Use one Git execution mode: worktree or current branch, never both.
Default to no automatic push, no merge, no force-push, no default-branch writes, no secrets, no destructive cleanup, and no personal or production data mutation.
Require a local artifact and commit ahead of the base; process exit code or repeated stop-condition text is not success proof.
Treat spawn failure, unsupported adapter, authentication-required, and explicit quota exhaustion as bounded preflight blockers rather than repeated iterations.
Keep application behavior in code and contracts; prompts orchestrate but are not the only implementation.
The final generated prompt must be ready to paste without combining text from another section.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Build or repair a repo-local AI harness and commit the implementation
prompts.use_this_when: A repository needs executable agent rules, maps, workflows, registries, validators, skills, capabilities, triggers, and proof gates before sustained autonomous work.
prompts.inspect_first: Existing repo rules, tests, scripts, manifests, hooks, generated-output policy, branch/PR conventions, and any partial harness.
prompts.expected_output: Committed harness spine and agent harness with deterministic routing, validators, fixtures, workflow contracts, operator documentation, and a clean handoff.
prompts.next_step: Use P47 to execute one committed harness workflow against a bounded product sprint.
prompts.proof_gate: A fresh agent can discover repo law, select a workflow, run validators, avoid known traps, register artifacts, commit results, and report the proof ceiling.
lines: gnhf `
--agent "xyz_proven_gnhf_agent_spec" `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 8 `
--max-tokens 800000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "The repo-local harness is implemented and committed, required validators pass or exact blockers are committed, generated artifacts are registered, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path
Sprint: Build or repair the repo-local AI harness.
Lane: harness spine, agent harness, integration seams, validation, and operator reporting.
Do not return a plan only. Inspect existing repository rules, tests, scripts, manifests, hooks, plans, validators, artifact policies, branch conventions, and partial harness files before inventing.

Build the smallest coherent harness that lets a fresh agent enter the repo, discover governing instructions, choose the correct workflow, call verified capabilities, respond to deterministic triggers, run required validators, register outputs, avoid forbidden mutations, commit useful work, and hand off cleanly.
Required factoring:
- harness spine: run context, artifact registry, workflow registry, schemas, fixtures, validators, proof vocabulary, proof gates, and final handoff compression
- agent harness: scoped skills, capabilities, triggers, routing, instruction surfaces, tool or MCP contracts, hooks, and context injection
- application boundary: keep product behavior in domain code and contracts rather than prompts
- integration seams: explicit interfaces between harness, agent routing, and application logic
For every skill, capability, and trigger, record owner, deterministic activation condition, inputs, outputs, preconditions, forbidden conditions, guardrails, tests, proof level, and likely files.
Retire or rewire stale guidance rather than preserving competing authority.
Reuse existing repo patterns. Do not create a second harness beside a working canonical harness.
Validation order:
1. parse schemas, manifests, and scripts
2. run focused harness contract tests
3. run trigger and capability fixtures
4. run existing repository validators
5. run build or static checks
6. run secret, generated-output, and git-diff hygiene checks
Commit coherent tracked changes. Do not push, merge, force-push, write to the default branch, expose secrets, delete unknown worktrees, or claim runtime proof from static checks.

Final response must include branch, commit SHA, files changed, harness factoring decisions, exact validations and results, artifacts, blockers, proof achieved, proof ceiling, final git status, and one exact next command."

prompts.sprint_path_role: Execute one committed harness workflow and advance product code with checkpoint proof
prompts.use_this_when: The repo-local harness exists and one registered workflow should perform a bounded product or repair sprint overnight.
prompts.inspect_first: Harness registries, selected workflow, run context, artifact contract, owned files, canonical validators, current git floor, and prior run evidence.
prompts.expected_output: One bounded workflow execution with registered artifacts, coherent checkpoint commits, validation evidence, updated run context, and an exact blocker when completion is unsafe.
prompts.next_step: Review commits and proof, then push or open/update the PR through a separate operator-authorized step.
prompts.proof_gate: The selected workflow is registered, inputs and authority are valid, each completed item has an artifact or code commit, canonical validation runs, and the worktree ends clean.
lines: gnhf `
--agent "xyz_proven_gnhf_agent_spec" `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 10 `
--max-tokens 1000000 `
--prevent-sleep on `

--stop-when "The selected registered workflow has completed its bounded queue or every remaining item has an exact committed blocker, required artifacts and validation evidence are registered, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path
Sprint: Execute one repo-local harness workflow.
Workflow ID: xyz_registered_workflow_id
Owned scope: xyz_owned_scope
Forbidden scope: xyz_forbidden_scope

Do not return a plan only. Read the repo law, run context schema, workflow registry, artifact registry, selected workflow, capability and trigger contracts, current branch and worktrees, recent commits, tests, validators, and prior run evidence.

Refuse to proceed when the workflow is unregistered, required inputs are missing, the worktree contains unknown partial work, mutation authority is absent, or the selected agent failed its exact spawn preflight.
Execute the workflow through repository code and contracts, not by replacing product behavior with prompt prose.
Use checkpoint commits for coherent completed items. Do not repeat an identical deterministic failure without a changed condition.
Register every required artifact with run ID, source inputs and hashes where allowed, producing workflow, validator profile, privacy class, proof level, and proof ceiling.
Run the workflow-specific validation order, then relevant repository validators, build checks, secret and generated-output hygiene, git diff --check, and final git status.
Do not push, merge, force-push, deploy, release, mutate personal or production state, weaken validators, hide failures, or claim a higher proof level than observed.
A successful process exit without the required artifact and commit is failure.

Final response must include workflow ID, run ID, branch, checkpoint commits, files and artifacts, exact validation results, completed and blocked queue items, proof achieved, proof ceiling, final git status, and one exact next command."
